data.text: Cuba appears to hold speedboat suspects in provincial hospital
data.snippet: Cuba on Thursday appeared to be holding at a guarded provincial hospital at least some of the six wounded exiles who were detained following what Cuba described as an attack by 10 heavily armed Cuban nationals who tried to infiltrate the country by speedboat.

image.text: People approach the entrance of the Provincial Clinical–Surgical Hospital "Arnaldo Milian Castro," where, according to local information, injured people were being treated after an armed incident involving a Florida-registered speedboat and a Cuban patrol, at a time of heightened tensions with the United States, in Santa Clara, Cuba, February 25, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.text: Cuba says it killed 4 exiles, wounded 6 others who attacked from Florida speedboat
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Cuban forces killed four exiles and wounded six others who sailed into Cuban waters aboard a Florida-registered speedboat on Wednesday and opened fire on a Cuban patrol, the Cuban government said at a time of heightened tensions with the United States.

relatedHeadlines.text: Russia says boat incident near Cuba is 'aggressive provocation' by U.S.
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Russia's foreign ministry spokeswoman said on Thursday that an incident with a Florida-registered speedboat off the coast of Cuba was an ‘aggressive provocation by the United States’ aimed at escalating the situation and triggering a conflict, state-owned TASS news agency reported.

relatedHeadlines.text: US to allow resale of Venezuelan oil to Cuba's private sector, Treasury says
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The U.S. Treasury Department said on Wednesday it would authorize companies seeking licenses to resell Venezuelan oil to Cuba's private sector, according to guidance posted on the department's website, a move that could help ease the island's acute fuel scarcity.

relatedHeadlines.text: Rubio urges Caribbean leaders to cooperate on gangs as Cuba worries grow
relatedHeadlines.dateLine: BASSETERRE, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Feb 25 (Reuters)
relatedHeadlines.snippet: U .S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Wednesday called for deeper cooperation on tackling criminal gangs as he met Caribbean leaders, even as regional concerns grew over the potential fallout from Washington's squeeze on Cuba.

data.text: US officials met Castro grandson in St. Kitts, Miami Herald reports
data.snippet: U.S. officials close to Secretary of State Marco Rubio met with the grandson of former Cuban leader Raul Castro on the sidelines of this week's regional Caribbean Community conference in St. Kitts and Nevis, the Miami Herald reported on Thursday.

image.text: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio gives a thumbs up as he boards his plane to return to Washington after meetings with Caribbean Community (CARICOM) leaders, from Robert L. Bradshaw International Airport in Basseterre, Saint Kitts and Nevis, February 25, 2026.

data.text: Ecuador to increase tariff on Colombian goods to 50% from March
data.snippet: Ecuador will increase tariffs on Colombian imports to 50% from the current 30% from March 1, the government said on Thursday, escalating a trade spat between the neighboring countries fueled by fears over border security.

image.text: High-voltage power lines of Colombia's national public energy grid stretch over a roadway as motorcyclists cross a bridge, in Yumbo, Colombia, February 2, 2026.

data.text: Bank of Mexico edges up 2026 economic growth forecast but flags risks
data.snippet: The Bank of Mexico on Thursday edged up its growth forecast for Mexico's economy this year, but flagged continued risks from global trade tensions ahead of this year's revision of Mexico's trade deal with the U.S. and Canada.

image.text: The logo of Mexico's central bank is seen at its building in downtown Mexico City, Mexico, April 26, 2024.

data.text: Canada says it could have bilateral critical sector deals with US
data.snippet: Canada is negotiating with the U.S. to remove tariffs on some critical sectors, and a deal could be wrapped into bilateral pacts alongside a review of the United States-Mexico-Canada free trade agreement, a Canadian senior minister said on Thursday.

image.text: Canada's Minister responsible for Canada–U.S. Trade and Intergovernmental Affairs and One Canadian Economy, Dominic LeBlanc, speaks as he and Mexico's Economy Minister Marcelo Ebrard (not pictured) attend the kickoff of the Team Canada Trade Mission to Mexico, at a hotel in Mexico City, Mexico, February 16, 2026.

data.text: EMERGING MARKETS-Colombian stocks lead Latin America shares lower; investors cautious about AI risk
data.snippet: Most Latin American currencies and stocks drifted lower on Thursday, with investors turning cautious about the AI trade. Colombian stocks slumped 2.9%, while the peso fell 1.5%, both touching their lowest points in over 1-1/2 months.

image.text: Traders work on the floor of the Colombian Stock Exchange in Bogota, Colombia May 9, 2024.

data.text: Venezuela fuel station operators declare emergency on minimal earnings-document, sources
data.snippet: Venezuela's association of gasoline station operators has declared an emergency in the sector because of what operators say are minimal earnings, sources and a document seen by Reuters showed.

data.text: Ecuadorean lawmakers pass reform geared at driving mining investment
data.snippet: Ecuador's National Assembly on Thursday approved reforms proposed by President Daniel Noboa aimed at boosting the nation's mining sector, but that have been criticized by environmentalists and indigenous groups.

data.text: Mexico's Salinas-controlled TV Azteca announces bankruptcy proceedings
data.snippet: Mexican broadcaster TV Azteca said on Thursday that shareholders had approved a voluntary bankruptcy process for restructuring the company's finances and liabilities.

image.text: The logo of broadcaster TV Azteca is seen outside its headquarters in Mexico City, Mexico June 14, 2024.

data.text: Mexico's Cemex to buy US stucco maker Omega in US building materials push
data.snippet: Mexico's Cemex will buy U.S. stucco manufacturer Omega Products International, the cement maker said on Thursday, as part of its push toward offering additional building materials in one of its largest markets.

image.text: A view shows the logo of Mexican cement maker CEMEX at its company's office building, in Monterrey, Mexico, January 27, 2026.

data.text: Venezuela top prosecutor Saab resigns, will serve as acting ombudsman
data.snippet: Venezuela Attorney General Tarek Saab has resigned after a nearly nine-year tenure, the country's legislature said on Wednesday, but has been elected as the country's acting ombudsman after that official also resigned.

image.text: Venezuela's Attorney General Tarek Saab speaks to Reuters during an interview, in Caracas, Venezuela, February 11, 2026.

data.text: US will not allow Venezuelan government to pay Maduro's legal fees, lawyer says
data.snippet: The U.S. is blocking the Venezuelan government from paying for Nicolas Maduro's legal representation in the drug trafficking case he faces in New York, the deposed Venezuelan president's defense lawyer said on Wednesday.

image.text: Venezuelan President Nicholas Maduro addresses members of CARICOM at a plenary session during the 40th Heads of government meeting at the Hilton Trinidad and Conference Centre July 6, 2013.

notifications.message: Your requested language with value en-GB is not supported, using en-us instead.
